name: autopoint
title: autopoint
summary: tool for setting up gettext infrastructure in a source package
description:
tool for setting up gettext infrastructure in a source package
The `autopoint' program copies standard gettext infrastructure files
into a source package.  It extracts from a macro call of the form
`AM_GNU_GETTEXT_VERSION(VERSION)', found in the package's
`configure.in' or `configure.ac' file, the gettext version used by the
package, and copies the infrastructure files belonging to this version
into the package.

arguments.description: print modifications but don't perform them
arguments.raw: --dry-run  print modifications but don't perform them

arguments.description: force overwriting of files that already exist
arguments.raw: -f  force overwriting of files that already exist

arguments.description: force overwriting of files that already exist
arguments.raw: --force  force overwriting of files that already exist

arguments.description: print modifications but don't perform them
arguments.raw: -n  print modifications but don't perform them

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use autopoint because tool for setting up gettext infrastructure in a source package.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use autopoint as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `autopoint [OPTION]...` as documented upstream for autopoint.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair autopoint with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `autopoint --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
